entry.title: SMB Adapter for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
While working with network file shares, you must come across a scenario where files must be exchanged between applications or systems via SMB servers. You can now utilize SAP’s latest SMB adapter to configure the connection to the SMB server to read files from the SMB server or create files or directories on the SMB server.

What is SMB?

The Server Message Block (SMB) protocol is a network file-sharing protocol for file-based operations like read, write, create, delete, etc.

What is the SMB Adapter?

Using the SMB adapter, you can easily integrate different SAP and non-SAP applications in an SMB network share environment. You can access files on a remote server securely using the latest 3.x SMB encryption protocols and the SMB Signing feature. You can improve file operations efficiency by enabling Distributed File Share.

Key Features

SMB Adapter can be used to implement various file-based solutions, a few of them are:

  • SMB Operations Support: Offers support for all major file operations: Create, Read, Write, List, and Delete files.
  • Dynamic configuration with headers and properties: Assigning dynamic values to different properties allows enhanced flexibility to your integration flows. You can also refer to dynamic parameters using SAP Cloud Integration exchange headers and properties.
  • Secure authentication and encryption for 3.x SMB dialects: Your communications over SMB network shares are done through secure and encrypted channels for all SMB dialects above 3.0.

How to use the SMB Adapter?

You can perform read and write operations with the required read lock strategy and file handling options respectively. Also, you can perform other important operations like listing files and directories, and creating or deleting files.

Create a File and List the Directory items

For the purposes of this blog, I am going to illustrate a basic operation of file creation on a remote folder in an SMB share. Secondly, we use the SMB adapter to list the contents of the folder to view properties of our newly created file.

The payload for SMB file operation

We use a Content Modifier to store folder and file details required for our SMB Adapter operations. The following properties are used later in the integration flow.

SMB Adapter Connection

You can connect to SMB using your network share credentials via a Secure Parameter artifact.

The connection tab contains the following fields:

Field

Description

Address

Specify the IP address and port name of the SMB Server.

Example: smb1.servershare.com:445

Domain

Specify the domain used for authentication.

SMB Dialect

Select the SMB dialect or version to be used for communication.

Share

Specify the SMB share folder. 

Authentication

Select the authentication type for connecting to the SMB server.

This field is set to Basic Authentication by default.

Credential Name

Specify the name of the User Credentials artifact used for authentication.

Timeout (in secs)

Specify the connection timeout duration to the SMB server.

Enable Encryption

Enable to turn on encrypted communication between the client and server. For any SMB 3.x dialect, this is a request to the server to turn on encryption if the server also supports it.

Enable DFS

Enable Distributed File System Share between the client and the server.

Enable Signing

Check to enable SMB Signing between the client and the server.

Maximum Reconnect Attempts

Specify the maximum number of retries permissible for reconnection to the SMB server before message processing starts.

This field is set to 3 by default.

Create File

Add a request-reply block to SMB Adapter to our flow. For file creation, you can utilize the correct File Handling Strategy.

List Directory Objects

After providing an interval of 180 seconds, we can initiate our call to list the directory objects.

Note: This adapter is compatible with Cloud Integration and EIC runtimes. In case, you want to connect with an SMB server in your organization's network then make use of the EIC runtime to run the integration scenario.

Quick Links

For detailed information about the adapter configuration, see the SMB Adapter Guide.

Note: The SMB Adapter is available as part of your SAP Integration Suite license.

entry.title: Re: How to add Partner ContactPerson to SalesOrder via API
entry.content:
Hi @trowbste,
This is not a bug in the OData V2 Service. In OData V2 Service for Sales Orders you need to use the language-dependent keys for partner function. Your issue is exactly the example in the documentation. If your logon language is english, you need to use "CP" for contact person. For german you would need to use "AP".

It is documented here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/5cbeeaca1d554da8a82e425e3e8bebf2.html

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Second Half 2024 Release: What's New in SAP SuccessFactors Time Management
entry.content:
It’s time to cover the 2024 second half SAP SuccessFactors Time Management release. In this blog, I’ll highlight key features from the 2H 2024 release, now available in preview and coming to production instances on November 15th. For full details, be sure to check out the 'What's New Viewer'.

Improving the time management experience for employees.

This release brings new capabilities for a more seamless employee and manager experience. Employees can now request time off directly within Microsoft Teams, simplifying the process by allowing employees to stay within the app they use most. The experience mirrors that within SAP SuccessFactors, maintaining data validations and alerts. Managers can also approve time-off requests in Teams, and employees can sync upcoming leave with their Microsoft Teams Calendar.


Request time off via Microsoft Teams​

Employees can use new calendar features in SAP SuccessFactors to manage their schedules more flexibly, enhancing work-life balance. They can self-propose their work schedule, reducing work for managers and HR. They can also request a temporary shift change, and even reschedule public holidays for future use in case of business travel or missed holidays. Validation rules help ensure compliance with contractual agreements, and built-in approval workflow ensures managers are informed and can approve requests.

Employee self-service work schedule change

Additionally, employees can now view time valuation warnings and errors directly in their mobile time sheet, making adjustments on the go. New custom fields for allowances also help improve compliance, which leads me to the next section of this blog.

Mobile alerting

Ensuring compliance and payroll accuracy.

In the last release, we introduced the Time Sheet Approval Center, designed to be the one-stop shop for managers to monitor and process time data for employees. This has been the most successful Restricted Availability program we’ve run to date. This 2H 2024 release makes it General Available and introduces new enhancements to help managers ensure HR regulatory compliance and minimize payroll errors. Amendments highlight any changes made by employees after a time sheet is approved and resubmitted, so managers can see all information at a glance. This makes it easier for managers to track changes and approve time sheets with confidence. Additionally, the mass approval capability boosts efficiency, helping managers handle approvals swiftly.  

Time Sheet Approval Center

We are also introducing a new time statement to help ensure compliance with HR regulations that require the publication of this information. This feature is particularly important in, but not limited to, Austria, Germany and Switzerland. The time statement helps employees quickly understand their time valuation results, including planned and worked hours, overtime, and absences. The templates are fully configurable and can be defined based on the employee recording profile. The statements can be emailed or saved as a PDF, which makes it widely accessible for all employee types.

Time statement

Last but not least, we are happy to introduce a new time sheet attestation feature, which lets employees confirm that their recorded hours are correct. The attestation messages can be customised by country and employee groups and hyperlink specific policies. This helps ensure compliance, especially in highly regulated industries. It also helps to ensure payroll accuracy, so employees are paid accurately and on time.

Timesheet attestation​

For a detailed review of everything included in the 2H 2024 Time Management release, be sure to check out Volker Ruof’s blog.

You can explore all the new enhancements for SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking and Employee Central Time Off in the 'What’s New Viewer.'

For additional release information and assets, visit the SAP SuccessFactors community page.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Core Development Tools 3.42.0 vs ABAP Core Devel...

Hi Ign,

may I please ask you to post the first characters of your trial instance you are using (e.g. 115f98b5)?

entry.title: Re: Creating a Functional Specification Document (FSD) in SAP: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content: This FSD blog is recommended exclusively for custom reports, export files, and dashboards.

entry.title: Re: Change of Landing Page domain in Marketing Cloud: No...
entry.content:
Dear Sören,

 if you publish a new Landing Page you will get a for the last two years a new domain. This URL shall be used in mail campaigns. If you unpublish your existing Landing Page and publish it again the domain changes also. Currently the old URL is working but we strongly recommend to use the new domain as the old one  leads to a deprecated technology.

Best regards Andreas Bettin

entry.title: Assistance Needed with URL Mashup App Error in SAP B1 Web Client
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I have recently started working on the URL Mashup App feature of the SAP B1 Web Client. Following the guide on How to Develop Web Client Extensions How to Develop Web Client Extensions , I generated the mtar file and deployed it using the SAP B1 Extension Manager.

Please note that this is a Fiori app hosted on BTP Cloud Foundry, and currently, the authentication mode in the app router is set to 'none'.

However, when I attempt to open the app in the SAP B1 Web Client, I encounter the following error:

Developer Tools Screenshot

Could you please assist me in resolving this issue?

Thank you.

Regards

Rahul Jain

entry.title: Re: Provision users from Microsoft Azure AD to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
entry.content:
Hello   @Matthew_Shaw 

You mentioned that it’s recommended  first to provision users from Azure to IAS and then from IAS to SAC (AZURE -> IAS -> SAC).

However, in SAP IAS, I cannot manage provisioned users (e.g., change name, phone number, etc.), as these modified parameters will be overwritten during the next IPS job. So, what is the advantage of storing users in IAS?

How can I even distinguish between provisioned users and users created directly in IAS? 

Isn’t it better to provision users directly from Azure to SAC and bypass IAS ?

 

Thank you very much for your support!

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS View for Customer and Contact
entry.content:
Dear Marc,

the RelationData view shows unidirectional from the contact person to the account and not in both directions.

Best regards Andreas Bettin

entry.title: openSAP Microlearning videos are now bite-sized learning videos on the SAP Learning site
entry.content:
Over many years, openSAP Microlearning has provided bite-sized, self-contained learning videos for customers, partners, and employees, helping everyone to stay up to date with the latest SAP technologies.

Continuing our mission to bring all SAP learning resources into one place, and helping to unify the user experience, we are pleased to announce that openSAP Microlearning has been integrated into the SAP Learning site. The most relevant and popular openSAP Microlearning videos have been migrated, and nearly 800 videos are now available free of charge as bite-sized learning videos on the SAP Learning site. New videos in this easy to consume bite-sized format are added to the SAP Learning site weekly, so check back often to see the latest content!

The openSAP Microlearning platform will be discontinued in early 2025. You can find the latest information about the migration on our FAQ page. Thank you to everyone who contributed to and learned with openSAP Microlearning over the past years. We’ll see you on the SAP Learning site!

What is the SAP Learning site? Designed to be simple and intuitive, the SAP Learning site offers free self-paced videos, courses, and learning journeys that enable people to quickly upskill, reskill, and stay up to date with key cloud and technology solution areas, as well as prepare for certification. Anyone with an internet connection can simply launch and learn. For deeper upskilling, the site also offers access to guided premium learning resources that are accessible with a subscription. Subscribed learners can access guided learning resources such as expert-led live sessions in key focus areas and topics. They can also access hands-on practice systems to gain practical skills and experiment in preconfigured system environments. The SAP Learning site aims to be the single destination for all learning needs.

entry.title: Re: MIRO: Planning level taken from the reconciliation account instead of the planning group.
entry.content: Problem still present , SAP support did not reply , i'd appreciate any tips to get over this problem

entry.title: Date getting stuck in story reports data queries
entry.content:
Hi 

Quite often the dynamic date of 'today' in my story report gets stuck and doesn't refresh every day as expected. 

When I investigate the data query, I confirm that all data tables are filtered to the time setting of - dynamic date - as of today. It is not obvious to me what could cause the date to be getting stuck. 

To fix the issue, I take the filter off and reapply and then the data refreshes to show what I would expect to see. 

This date issue is affecting all story reports but not consistently. Example, one report with dynamic date as today will get stuck but a similar report on the same day will be fine. The reports are all structured in a similar way with most of them beginning with 'Job Information'. 

Please can I get advise on how I can ensure my story reports with dynamic dates do not get stuck ? 

Thanks 
Aisling

entry.title: Replacement of function module - SD_CREDIT_EXPOSURE in S/4 Hana
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

The customer was using the function module SD_CREDIT_EXPOSURE in the ECC system. However, its obsolete in S/4HANA. If you may please suggest the replacement of this function module in S/4HANA. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Replacement of function module - SD_CREDIT_EXPOSURE i...
entry.content:
Hello,
 
Function modules CREDIT_EXPOSURE and SD_CREDIT_EXPOSURE were reading data from S066, S067 infostructures and KNKK table. These are no longer relevant on S/4HANA system with SAP Credit Management (FSCM). The SD values are stored directly in table UKM_ITEM with liability category (100 for open orders, 400 for open deliveries, 500 for open billing documents).  FI data (liability category 200 and 300) in the standard implementation is 'directly read', so the related FI documents are on the fly selected and not stored in UKM_ITEM itself. 
 
To read the FSCM credit exposure the function module UKM_COMMTS_READ can be used. The output values are listed in ET_UKM_TOTALS structure per liability category.
There is no other replacement for the ECC BAPIs to read credit exposure data on S/4HANA releases.

Keep in mind that neither of the mentioned function modules are released. Issues arising through the usage of such FMs is the responsibility of customer.
 
Best regards,
Edina
SAP

entry.title: Re: Mass update of UKMBP_CMS_SGM-CREDIT_LIMIT
entry.content: bp3100 cannot be saved with that logic.

entry.title: SAP Fiori FES4.0 upgrade to FES 2023
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are on Hub deployment of SAP Fiori FES4.0 on AS ABAP 7.52 currently. Backend is S/4HANA 1709. We are upgrading S/4HANA 1709 to 2023 and want to upgrade to FES server also to FES 2023. Please suggest if a direct upgrade is possible. I have checked PAM, notes, blogs but could not find answer.

 

TIA

Regards

Pankaj

entry.title: SAP SD pricing requirement routine dynamic changes
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using customized pricing routine in SD pricing procedure to restrict populating one condition. This  tax related Z condition should be active if only certain tax code is selected. The tax code is available in XKOMV structure and the routine sets SUBRC with correct value if the very first time the condition is met (that is when the sales order is being created and pricing is set the first time.) any subsequent changes to the sales order still in create mode which may change Tax code does not dynamically sets the condition ON or OFF. On debugging, we found that the subrc variable gets correct value and the routine also executes the correct flow. But the condition remains unimpacted with On-the-Go changes. meaning, once popualated, it does not get removed if the SUBRC is set 4 from routine.

any suggestion ? I am posting code omitting all declaration/remarks.

FORM kobed_940.

*    In case of Condition Item Tax unavailable, check TAX code on Condition Structure
  READ TABLE xkomv[] INTO l_wa_komv_mwst WITH KEY kposn = komp-kposn
                                                  kschl = l_con_kschl_mwst.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
*       Populate Tax code available on Runtime Condition Structure
    l_var_mwsk1 = l_wa_komv_mwst-mwsk1.
  ENDIF.
  sy-subrc = 4.
  IF l_var_mwsk1 IS NOT INITIAL.    "INS ONE-15726
    IF komk-vbtyp = 'H'  OR              "Returns
           komk-vbtyp = 'K'  OR
  sy-subrc = 0.

  ENDIF.              
ENDIF.
*} 

ENDFORM.                    "KOBED_940

FORM kobev_940.
    sy-subrc = 0.
 ENDFORM
We have also tried repeating the same code from KOBED in KOBEV but with no result. Pls guide.

thanks

Binita

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud - Landing Page Favicon
entry.content:
Hello Brad,
Could you find a solution for this requirement? 
Kind regards
Sören

entry.title: 2H 2024 Release Highlights of SAP Best Practices for SAP SuccessFactors Time Management
entry.content:
Dear All,

Hope you are doing fine.

I am excited to share the new features that we are planning to deliver as part of the Best Practices package for Time Management 2H 2024 via the upgrade centre. We have some new features (new country localizations) along with enhancements for existing countries. We actually delivered a lot of new features in this release, and this is what makes it exciting for those of you who are looking for any accelerated implementations.

 Disclaimer: Please note these features are currently available only on preview. The final version will be updated on RTC or production date for 1H 2024.

 

Time Forms ESS (Employee Self Services)

In 1 H 2024 we had released Time Forms Content which includes pre-delivered templates for Maternity, Strike letters etc. In 2H 2024, we are enhancing the same with role-based permissions (RBP) for employees.

The employees would be able to request letters using the pre-delivered templates now.

Enhancement to Absence Counting Rule for India

 

In India we see customer cases who has 3 different type of work schedule day models.

  1. Half Day – 4 hours
  2. Full Day – 8 hours
  3. Extended Day – 12 hours

Employees work 5 days / 40 hours a week. An employee can be assigned a Work Schedule which looks like something like below:

Sun

Mon

Tues

Wed

Thurs

Fri

Sat

Week Off

Full Day

Full Day

Extended Day

Half Day

Full Day

Weekly Off

To cater to above, we have now delivered absence counting rules that counts in below manner:

  1. On Monday, Tuesday or Friday – 1 day leave balance is deducted.
  2. On Wednesday – 1.5 day leave balance is deducted.
  3. On Thursday – 0.5 day leave balance is deducted.

For more details and to check the rules, please use the India Time off Best Practices package from the upgrade centre.

Netherlands Time Off Enhancements

We redesigned some of the accrual rules and time account types for Netherlands 2H 2024 based on the feedback received from some of our partners.  I don’t want to digress from the topic here but as a partner you can have a say in our best practices design. If you want to influence our best practices content or provide feedback on existing countries, please feel free to reach out to us on sfbestpractices.support@sap.com . We would be more than happy to connect with you.

Now coming back to NLD topic, what has changed?

Group statutory and non-statutory Leaves in One bucket.

Earlier Statutory and Non-Statutory leaves were separate leave or time types. Now these are two-time account types attached to the same time type. Annual Leave is the time type mapped to these two-time account types.

Deduction order:

Statutory   TAT (Booking range 1.5 years)

Non statutory TAT (Booking range 5 years)

Statutory Time Account Type (TAT)

Example: 2024: Jan 2024 – June 2025 (within overlapping accounts ensure oldest first by validity)

                2025: Jan 2025 – June 2026

 

Non statutory Time Account Type (TAT)

Example:

Jan 2024 - Dec 28

Jan 2025 – Dec 29

Additional Notes:

Non-Statutory Part – Some customers do not use seniority. In terms of Hours, this translates to

160 hours => Statutory 

40 hours (5 days * say 8 hours) => Non-Statutory

The most common is above and is be used for Best Practices

A new time profile Generic Profile2 (NLD) is delivered to handle this scenario.Further we are also taking care of fixed term contracts that have a contract end date. The accruals for these employees are also prorated based on contract end date.

 

Removal of custom fields in Job Info for Accruals

We currently don’t use custom field (which was used earlier in averaging FTE for accruals). We use directly average FTE rule function in accruals rules.

For more details, please use the Netherlands Time off Best practices package from upgrade center

 

Short Term Disability for United States

We introduced content for STD or Short-term disability leave for united states which includes different disability periods as per absence pay policy. The absence pay policy and scheme is also part of pre delivered content.

With pre-delivered content, the disability period dates are automatically set based on absence pay scheme and policy whenever a STD leave is requested. The leave request along with these dates are sent to payroll for payroll processing. Different periods have different pay percentages like fully paid, 70% paid etc. depending on the payroll policies.

Here are some of the screenshots of the absence pay scheme and policy.

For more details, please use the United States Time off Best practices package from upgrade center

Seniority Calculation Policy in Accruals rules of United Kingdom or GB

We are now making use of Seniority Calculation Policy and its corresponding rule function in Accruals Rules of GB. Before this we used to make use of “Get number of calendar years” Rule function to derive the seniority of an employee

The Seniority Calculation Policy Object from EC is a more sophisticated configuration which can handle more complex use cases like excluding some unpaid absences from total seniority etc.

In the sample, we deliver a simple seniority config based on years from hire date. But it can be extended further based on your needs. Here are some of the screenshots related to the best practices content for seniority.

Accrual Rule:

For more details, please use the SAP Best Practices Employee Central Time Off (United Kingdom) from upgrade center.

Time Tracking Sweden

We have now introduced Time Tracking Sample solution for Sweden to cater to white collar employees for now. The requirements are a bit complicated, and I had to write around 100-time valuations to achieve this. Yes, you heard it right approx. 100-time valuations!!!. This is the biggest time tracking /time sheet package that we have ever built

The configurations and possible extensions with explanation of logic is present in our workbooks and you can refer them for more details.

 

The below are some of the requirements and explanations.

 

Example 1:

An employee has a works from 8 AM to 5 PM

Employee Records OT directly after the shift end i.e starting from 5 PM

There is immediate OT paid as 1.5 only for 1 hour

 

 

Example 2

Recording: 6 PM to 7 PM

 

 

So Delta is 2 hours essentially extension from 7PM to 9 PM to match min 3 HR as guaranteed hours.

So, DELTA 1.5 is 1 HR and Delta 2.0 is also 1 HR.

If you are interested to know more, please use upgrade center to upgrade Best Practices for Time tracking Sweden

 

Time Tracking Finland

 

We have also now delivered a set of Time Valuation rules to handled daily overtime, weekly overtime, Sunday, and Holiday Work for Finland.

We cover both 40 Hour employees and 37.5 Hour employees. In addition , rest period handling is also taken care. As part of rest period, the employee needs to rest for a minimum of 11 hours between two consecutive shifts

If you are interested to know more, please use upgrade center to upgrade Best Practices for Time tracking Finland.

Example :

Scenario: Recording on a week with a holiday

Weekly Overtime = (Recorded Prod Time – planned time – daily OT)

= 72-40-20

= 32-20

= 12 as seen above

 

These best practices add a lot of value in terms of accelerating your implementations and I hope to see more adoption from you (partners) in coming days

I wish you good luck with implementations.

In case you have feedback, please do leave a comment below.

Thanks & Best Regards

Neelesh

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Hi @hemant2004 

Thank you for your engagement and preparing this valuable information. In order to meet the right audience I requested via ticket to move this information to the Technology Blogs by Members board 
This is the place to gain new perspectives and knowledge about enterprise resource planning in blog posts from community members. There members can explore a vibrant mix of technical expertise, industry insights, and tech buzz in member blogs covering SAP products, technology, and events.
Our SAP Learning Group Q&A board  board shall be used to ask and answer questions about SAP Learning e.g.  self-paced courses Learning Journeys, bite-sized learning videos, live sessions, hands-on practice SAP Certifications  and other assets accessible on SAP Learning

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Scripting (Part 2)
entry.content:
Hello,

I still can't see the point of these scripts, but I already left my 2 cents in part 1 of this blog. 

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: Develop Fiori app and deploy it into SAP backend system using SAP Business application studio.
entry.content:
Thank you for your contribution.
But ask yourself whether this blog post is worth publishing here.
Several points speak against it:

  • No explanation of the whys and wherefores, and only one-liners for the screenshots.
  • You are using BOPF and not RAP, no explanation of why.
  • You are using view (which is deprecated) and not ‘view entity’, no explanation of why.
  • There are numerous explanations of how to get to the things explained in the screenshots.

In addition, there are numerous tutorials from SAP (like this) that explain this in more detail and better.
I recommend deleting this post and trying again with a much better post.

entry.title: Re: While updating the Batch for Item system throwing an error
entry.content: Hello, the SAP note 2668383 has been removed.

Ratana

entry.title: Re: While updating the Batch for Item system throwing an error
entry.content: Hi Sir Nidhi,We have also encountered this error during upgrade. As per SAP Note 1250867, we should Complete the operation

entry.title: Re: While updating the Batch for Item system throwing an error
entry.content: Hi Sir Nidhi, We have also encountered this error during upgrade. As per SAP Note 1250867, we should Complete the operation under Batch Management. But we encountered this error No matching records found 'Inventory Transactions Log' (OITL) (ODBC -2028) [Message 131-183]

entry.title: Link sales orders to contracts in bulk for Data Migration
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have query regarding data migration. Actually, during Sales order migration, we are not able to link the sales order contract number in the sales order. There is no field for the contract in the template of the sale order. We have uploaded the Sales order via migration cockpit. In order to, link the Contract number in the Sales Order we have planned to use LSMW but unfortunately it is not working properly. 

It will be very hard to do this activity manually from VA02 since the volume is high. 

So, can we add the sales contract of a sales order in bulk?

Thank you.

Yes, it goes individually and after restoring a fully encrypted database the same encryption key must be used. Please see the documentation below.

Encrypting an existing database in ASE.

entry.title: Re: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q4 2024
entry.content: I can only agree with the other customers. The bi-weekly blogs about updates were always the basis for our test in the preview tenant and also the basis for the information sent to our key users. The Whats-New Viewer is not suitable for this.

entry.title: Re: Fill in a survey on behalf
entry.content: Thanks for your feedback. But the idea was that the responses obtained on paper would be included in the report statistics.

entry.title: Re: How to create Mandatory Variable at Story level in SA...
entry.content:
Hi @Sumalini,

variables depend on the type of model you´re using. Let´s assume you have a BW live connection: Here you simply define the variable in the BW query and set it to mandatory. If you then use this model in a story, you´ll get a variable pop-up as designer and are able to define, if you want to set a fixed value or show the pop-up for every user.

Based on your question, I guess you´re using an import model.I´d personally not use variables for your case, but I´ll describe it anyways below. But first here´s an option I do prefer to do (which does require scripting):

  • On your first page you do set all elements to "Data Refresh: Always pause"
  • Additionally set up an "OnInitialization" Event. This event opens a page pop-up (attention: story pop-ups can´t be always used in the OnInitialization event, since the pop-up information may not be loaded yet. Since I did not have this issue with page pop-ups, I currently stick to them).
  • The pop-up includes the filter you require. If you do require some mandatory filtering, you might want to add a check in your "Pup-up close"-button, if the filtering is OK.
  • If this filter should be used for the whole story, you could either use scripting to set story filters or save the selected values in script variables and use them in the "onActive" method for each page to look up the filters and set them by script per page.
  • Of course you need to set all elements to "Data Refresh: Always refresh" again by script when closing the popup.

Anyways: Here´s the info about model variables:

As soon as you create a model variable in the model - and use this model in your story, of course - you´ll see the option to set this variable:

But please keep in mind, that - beside switching the value of the variable - you can´t really do anything with the variable in the story.

Let´s assume you have model with the dimensions "Date" and  "TestDimension" and the measure "Value". You can now set up a variable like this:

This variable can then be used in a calculated measure within the model:

If you upload the following data:

DateTestDimensionValue
01.01.2024A1
01.02.2024B2
01.03.2024C4
01.04.2024A8
01.05.2024B16
01.06.2024C32
01.07.2024A64
01.08.2024B128
01.09.2024C256
01.10.2024A512
01.11.2024B1024
01.12.2024C2048

 and use the model data in the story, the result will be:

If you now switch the varible value in the story to be, the calculated measure will only show the value of "1170" in the row of TestDimension "B":

When you do add the model to the story, you do get the following pup-up:

So you can decide whether the user should get the pop-up whenever the story is opened.

Please keep in mind, that model variables does not offer any setting for mandatory / optional.

You can at least check the value of the selected variables using a script like:

 

var mv_TestVar1Value = Table_1.getDataSource().getVariableValues("mv_TestVar1");

 

Best regards,
Denis

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content: Hello @Fnsantos the proctor is only communicating in English language but we are working on a solution to offer more languages. The exam itself you can translate into several languages using Instant Translate within the exam. Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Report or Tool for Deleting Controlling Transaction Data in SAP S/4HANA (Release 2023) Test Systems
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

Is there a standard report or tool available in SAP S/4HANA (Release 2023) to delete Controlling-relevant transaction data from a test system, similar to the functionality provided by the SAPF020 report (transaction OBR1) for removing Financial Accounting transaction data?

 

Thank you for your support in advance.

Kind regards

Emal

entry.title: Re: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA 2021 – Part 1
entry.content:
Hello Christian,

thx for the blog, very helpful, but one question: we migrate at the moment from S/4HANA on prem to RISE  S/4HANA privat edition 2023 FSP02. When we activate the product compliance function , which version will be get?

I read in the blog from Johannes Reichel 2022

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-knowledge-base/product-compliance-transition-to-sap-s-4hana/ta-p/9677

that there is an option to choose the classic function instead of the "new" function, which is quite closer to the old EH&S and the functionality that we used today. But in the Info day 2023

 https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/sap-ehs-productcompliance-infodays-2023/pdfs/d1_product_compliance_solution_strategy_overview_coenning.pdf

 Ralf Coennig talk only about "new" function set and the classic is not longer more available ?

Is that true ? or what should we do to get the Classic function back ?

regards Oliver

entry.title: Re: Where can a database administrators start learning SAP HANA user management?
entry.content:
Hi @F_M 
We have several courses available on SAP Learning related to this topic SAP Business ByDesign courses 
The SAP Business ByDesign Administration covers how to manage SAP Business ByDesign, including setting up an organizational structure, extending the solution, and importing/exporting data. Topics include organizational management, task management, data import/export, solution extension, data protection, privacy, and information management from an administrator's perspective. System demonstrations are provided throughout the course for a clearer understanding of the solution.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Serial number prefix in MM
entry.content:
Hi Members 

I have the challenge that I need to add a prefix to my serial numbers, when I create a new purchase order, so far I have created a new category and a number range.  

I also created a serial number profile in MM and maintained my prefix, the issue is, that when I create a new PO in ME21N, the correct number range are picked but without prefix, it is important that the supplier got the information about the prefix, so how to show the serial number incl. prefix in ME21N and the output form?

Any ideas?

entry.title: Application for SAP Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hello @SAPCommunityTeam,

I’m Abdul Afzal, a Software Engineer specializing in SAP Basis, and I’m currently preparing for the SAP S/4HANA Implementation Technical Consultant certification. I’m excited to engage with the SAP Digital Skills Initiative and connect with professionals who share a passion for SAP and technology.

With a background in software engineering and a dedicated focus on SAP Basis, I’m also expanding my expertise in system administration, Fiori administration, and HANA administration. I’m eager to take on new challenges, share insights, and learn from this incredible community.

Thank you for this opportunity, and I look forward to meaningful connections and growth!

Best regards,
Abdul Afzal

entry.title: Re: how to display error message for choosing incorrect C...

entry.title: Storage Capacity Forecast Overview based on Production Output
entry.content:
Dear Community,

we have a requirement coming from the business regarding a forecast for the storage utilization based on the output from production and the current stock level. The background to the requirements is to see what the storage capacity will look like in the next few days / weeks based on the current stock level and the expected output of production orders or process orders.

Does someone know if there is a standard report available for that requirement?

It is requested for planning purposed in the operational horizon.

entry.title: Re: Best Practices - PP/PI ePPDS Ressource Naming Convention
entry.content: We decided to go with a 2digits for Department, 3digits for manufacturer of the resource and 3 digits for year of implementation as well a upcounting number to avoid resources with the same manufacturer and same implementation year.

entry.title: Understanding LU-Matricule Numbers and Peppol in the SAP solution context
entry.content:
In the area of electronic invoicing, the Peppol network plays a crucial role in ensuring seamless and standardized communication between businesses. For companies operating in Luxembourg, the use of Peppol identifiers is essential for the successful transmission of electronic invoices. This blog delves into the intricacies of the Luxembourg Matricule Number, Peppol network, and the SAP solution, providing a detailed workaround for companies facing issues with the mapping of participant IDs.

SAP provides a solution called SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, to enable customers in the Peppol network.

Background

Companies that use the Peppol Exchange process in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition often encounter challenges when invoicing their business partners in Luxembourg, particularly with the mapping of the Matricule Number. The Peppol identifier, a unique number associated with each entity, is used to reliably identify the sender and recipient of a message within the Peppol network. Without a Peppol ID, the network cannot determine the recipient of the invoice. SAP does not yet provide a standard solution, as "9938" is reserved for Luxembourg VAT numbers only, as indicated by Peppol Code Lists - Participant identifier schemes

It is common practise in Luxembourg that organizations can use either the VAT number or the 11-digit identity number of the directory of legal entities (also known as the Matricule Number) as their Peppol identifier. The format for these identifiers is as follows:

  • VAT Number: 9938:lu10061242
  • Matricule Number: 9938:12345678910

Issue and Solution

Customers have reported issues with the mapping of the participant ID when using the Matricule Number. Despite following the steps outlined in SAP Note 3057837, this solution was not sufficient to address the right recipient in Luxembourg with their Matricule Number.

Proposed solution

To address this issue, a solution can be implemented, which varies in SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA. There is no solution available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, at the moment.

For SAP S/4 HANA

  1. Transaction SM30 -> EDOEUPARTYV
    • Select "LU:VAT", copy the entry and rename it to "ZLU:MATR".
    • For Description, enter "Luxembourg Matricule Number"
    • For the "Mapping Method Outbound" field, enter "READ_PEPPOL_ID".
    • Clear the fields "Mapping Class Inbound" and "Mapping Method Inbound" (this solution is for outgoing customer invoices).
  2. Business Partner Master Data: Maintain the Matricule Number as a generic Peppol ID for your Business Partner, according to SAP Documentation.

For SAP ERP

  1. Transaction SM30 -> EDOEUPARTYV
    • Select "LU:VAT", copy the entry and rename it to "ZLU:MATR".
    • For Description, enter "Luxembourg Matricule Number".
    • For the "Mapping Method Outbound" field, enter "READ_TAX_NUMBER2".
    • Clear the fields "Mapping class inbound" and "Mapping Method Inbound" (this solution is for outgoing customer invoices).
  2. Populate the Tax Number 2 in the master data of your customer in the XD02 transaction, Control Data > Tax Information (similar to SAP Documentation).

Common Steps for SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP

"ZLU:MATR" can then be selected in Transaction SM30 -> EDOBUPAV.

The output will include the <Receiver> tag with the Matricule Number as shown below:

 

<n0:StandardBusinessDocument xmlns:n0=http://www.unece.org/cefact/namespaces/StandardBusinessDocumentHeader xmlns:prx="urn:sap.com:proxy:..." xmlns:n1="urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:CommonAggregateComponents-2" xmlns:n2="urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:CommonBasicComponents-2" xmlns:n3="urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:Invoice-2">

<n0:StandardBusinessDocumentHeader>

<n0:HeaderVersion>1.0</n0:HeaderVersion>

<n0:Sender>

<n0:Identifier Authority="iso6523-actorid-upis">{Peppol Sender ID}</n0:Identifier>

</n0:Sender>

<n0:Receiver>

<n0:Identifier Authority="iso6523-actorid-upis">9938:{Matricule Number}</n0:Identifier>

</n0:Receiver>

<n0:DocumentIdentification>

<n0:Standard>urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:Invoice-2</n0:Standard>

...

 

Conclusion

While this approach provides a temporary solution for customers facing issues with the mapping of participant IDs for Luxembourg, SAP does not yet provide a standard solution. The Peppol Participant Identifier Scheme “LU:VAT” / "9938" is reserved for VAT numbers only, as indicated by Peppol Code Lists - Participant identifier schemes

For more information how this handled in common practise, refer to the Dossiers and FAQs - E-facturation - Luxembourg.

Further References

  • Dossiers and FAQs - E-facturation - Luxembourg
  • Peppol Code Lists - Participant identifier schemes
  • Party ID Types for Receivers by Country/Region

Contributors

Special thanks to @monika_wolf , @simon_tacke and @Linda_Garcia  for your review and feedback!

entry.title: Re: Sales Document is always empty in Fiori App F4111. Is it possible to display values here?
entry.content:
You may check https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/c8fef622eed042fba2d0ddb749bac7ac.html for the supported standard fields in F4111. 'Sales document' becomes available in higher release. But 'payment terms' is still not available in F4111 yet.

As a workaround, you can create your own field following the instructions given in the attachment of SAP note 3261750 - Manage Prices – Sales app: Extensibility for standard fields

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori FES4.0 upgrade to FES 2023
entry.content: If this HUB FIORI is only used to access the S/4HANA that is being upgraded, then the recommendation is to go for a embedded deployment.

entry.title: Re: F1523 not show shifts from PPDS
entry.content: SAP Note 2991990

entry.title: Re: How to trigger a custom e-mail for Automatic Payment Advice ? And How to debug Automatic Payment
entry.content: Thanks for writing this blog, its very informative and helpfull

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Rep task - structure change in source table without Reconcile option
entry.content: As I know, the "initial + realtime with structure" option in replication doesn't handle structure changes during a replication task. It's meant for initial data load and real-time updates. For handling structure changes, like adding or removing columns in the source table, you can possibly use the "Reconcile Changes" option. Without it, the replication task might fail and needing manual adjustments. HANA Artefacts HDI Reptask refers to SAP HANA tools for setting up and managing replication tasks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud - Landing Page Favicon
entry.content: Hi Sören - no, unfortunately.

entry.title: Recruitment Reporting- Candidate Application Questions and answers
entry.content:
Hi 

I need to report on the candidate application questions and answers.


We can pull the questions from the backend of the system but this does not come with formatting features and therefore the back end report is not suitable for the end user i.e. hiring manager/recruitment team. 

I have referred to KBA 2749268 which advises that two reports need to be downloaded via the Integration Centre and then merged together.

Please provide an update on when the report centre will be able to report on candidate application questions, whether it be a table, canvas or story report ? 

Thanks 
Aisling

entry.title: how to use standard gender field in custom MDF as this is hardcode doesnt have any valid source
entry.content:
Hello All we have requirement where i need  to compare the gender personal info field based on the country from custom MDF.I have created the custom MDF with field gender what could be the valid source for this field so that can compare with standard gender field as the this field is hardcode value derived by the system.

 

Thanks

Sachin

entry.title: Re: MB90 is not generating SP02 Spool but Fiori app is ab...
entry.content:
It seems there is an issue with MB90 generating the SP02 spool, so may the below steps helps:

Check the output configuration for WF02 in NACE. Ensure settings like output medium, partner function, and communication strategy are correct.
Verify or create an output condition record for the material document and WF02 in MN04.
Ensure the proper printer/spool server is defined and active in SPAD.
Check that the spool request number range and settings in SPAD are correct and not deleting the request immediately.
Look for errors or warnings in the job logs in SM37 related to MB90.
Compare the Fiori app and MB90 processing steps to find differences.

If the issue persists, contact SAP support or open a support ticket with all relevant details.

entry.title: Re: Anomaly Forecast of Sensor Data in Energy Intensive Industries – Part II: The Machine Learning E
entry.content: Great blog! Super useful diagrams. 👌

entry.title: Re: Custom Fields and Logic: S4CRM Lead/Opportunity to Quote
entry.content: You can try implementing CRM_LORD2_BADI method IF_EX_CRM_LORD2~MAP_OPP_DATA.

entry.title: Re: Test and Production SAP ECC
entry.content:
The E070 table in SAP stores system change options for each client, including system type (test or production) and system ID.

To check if test and production environments are configured in SAP ECC:

  1. Log in to SAP ECC with appropriate authorization.
  2. Go to transaction SE16 (Data Browser) or SE16N (General Table Display).
  3. Enter "E070" in the Table field and click Execute.
  4. View the entries in the E070 table.
  5. Find entries with your SAP ECC system ID.
  6. Check the "SYST" field: "T" means a test system, "P" means a production system.

This should help you identify if test and production environments are set up in SAP ECC.

entry.title: Re: No active outbound service for appl. obj. 500000002620230001, outp. type MMIM_MATDOC_GDSMVMT_ESO
entry.content:
Hi,
have you assigned the business partners to your communication system ?

entry.title: Re: No active outbound service for appl. obj. 50000000262...
Re: Debug new for loop in ABAP 7.4 onwards syntax
entry.content: For information, it's not only in the "latest GUI version", "Step Size" used to exist for a very long time (at least 20 years). Before 7.40 it was used specifically to debug several statements in the same line of code.

entry.title: Re: using SAP API to export BPMN to SGX format
entry.content:
Hi, 
this is the newest API documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/sap-signavio-process-manager-api/import-and-export 
You need an S-User to access this resource. This describes how you import a BPMN via API, however we don't offer a Public endpoint for exporting the SGX. You can engage with our consulting team if you need help. I will log this feedback that such an Public API would be valuable, but there is nothing planned as of now. Can you please share more details about the use case you try to achieve?

Thank you and best regards
Manuel

entry.title: Re: Issue with Using Gateway OData V4 Services in Custom ABAP Deployment Problem
entry.content:
Hey, I am having the same issue while deploying SAP UI5 Application to BAS Fiori Launchpad.
Did you solve it?

entry.title: Re: can you use a CAL S4HANA with SAP BTC?
entry.content: Hello, SAP BTC is not integrated with SAP CAL. They both have conversion functionality, but you cannot use SAP BTC to make the transformation and SAP CAL to create the target infrastructure and shell system installation. Moreover I do not recommend using SAP CAL conversion scenario as it is going to be deprecated with the next SAP CAL release. You should use SAP BTC only. Regards, Nikola

entry.title: Re: How to send EWM Goods Issue from DM API
entry.content:
Hi, see: https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_wip/path/goodsIssueRequestPOST

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: How to enable the assessable value field for Non Indian customers in Public cloud.
entry.content: In case of VAT how to enable the assessable value field in SAP MM Public cloud system. Please help on the same.

entry.title: Re: Custom RAP Application Deployment to SAP S/4 HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Hi @SaiBhargav396 

It’s not entirely clear what you need and what the errors are. If you need the application to work, simply remove “-api” from the generated URL and run it.

If you need to add the application to the launchpad, then you need to:

In the App Manage launchpad space create new space and page

Open the role and assign the created space

Go to maintain launchpad pages
– open the created page
– Edit
– add description and choose the business catalogue by clicking add

 

Now you can test preview from "page preview"

 


BR, 
Oleg

entry.title: Re: Configure print service entitlements
entry.content:
Hi,

Prerequisites are described at Printing Guide: https://help.sap.com/doc/c62392b1706d407d9939f49f819acce0/latest/en-US/8140a27def1a4c21bef4ca29544b16b1.pdf

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: [Error] BDC clearing partial vendor field not input
entry.content: Note that you have colored in red the wrong line (positioning the cursor is permitted for non-input fields). The one which produces the error is the one which enters the value 100000.00-.

entry.title: Re: [Error] BDC clearing partial vendor field not input
entry.content: oki thank sir, i repaired

entry.title: Re: [Error] BDC clearing partial vendor field not input
entry.content: If you run in mode 'E', does it display a screen? If yes, it means that there is an error in the screen that you can bypass by pressing Enter. You will also get this error in modes N and P but that will cancel the execution instead of bypassing the error. In all cases, you have to check why the field is protected against input.

entry.title: Re: [Error] BDC clearing partial vendor field not input
entry.content: When i run mode 'E', it does not show error and it will create FI doc, Not problem.

entry.title: Re: Custom RAP Application Deployment to SAP S/4 HANA Pub...

entry.title: Re: Configure print service entitlements

entry.title: Re: Completing the Digital Blueprint - with the System Scan
entry.content: Hi, to use BTC, the target system has to be set up in a specific way (shell creation/conversion). More details are described in Lean Selective Data Transition Best Practices in section Shell Creation and Conversion Process . Best regards, Christiane

entry.title: Single IBD for Multiple PO with HU by IDoC DELVRY07
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Business has a requirement, where in the single inbound ASN IDoC DESADV (Basic type DELVRY07), multiple PO details in the E1EDL41-BSTNR will be send and for those PO, IBD should get posted. This scenario is working , but when we add HU details in the E1EDL37 (single or Multiple HU) for PO line items, we are getting below 3 error messages, 

-Packing not possible since there is no quantity to be packed

-Error while generating packaging data

-Delivery item cannot be exactly determined

Has any one come across such issues and if yes, how it's fixed. Can you please let us know.

Thank you.

entry.title: Communication arrangement in SAP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are facing connection issue while creating communication arrangement in SAP,

issue: SOAP:1032 SRT: Wrong Content-Type and empty HTTP-B ody received: ("HTTP Code 401 : Unauthorized")

we tried changing user ID and password but it's still the same.

 Please suggest us on how to solve this error.

Regards

Pranavi Reddy

entry.title: Develop ALV report based on user input
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need to develop ALV report given in attached image, here target value we fetch from table but achieved value will be entered by user, after entering by user need to calculate MTD% = Target Value / Achieved Value. The resulting calculations for both order and sales need to be saved in database table. some companies have all the inputs from user( Achieved and Target values entered by user). How this kind of report can be developed please suggest steps followed, Usually in ALV we display records column wise or row wise, here its a different case how to develop this

entry.title: IMPORT VAT will be calculated based on CIF and Basic custom Duty
entry.content:
Hello Team

Our client has import procurement scenario where import VAT will calculate on CIF value plus basic custom duty. We have not achieved any solution for this so please advice accordingly.

We asked for Assessable value field as editable from SAP but they said it is for India customers only.

 

How to achieve solution for this. Please reply

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Thank you for the warm welcome! I'm thrilled to join this vibrant space where SAP enthusiasts can connect and collaborate. I’m Omair, an SAP professional with over 10 years of experience across SAP SD, TM, and IS-Oil, and I’m excited to dive into discussions, learn from the community, and share insights from my journey. It’s always inspiring to meet others who are as passionate about SAP as I am. Looking forward to contributing, learning, and connecting with fellow members here!

entry.title: How to perform transformations in the SLT system?
entry.content:
In this blog post we will discuss on how we can perform conversions, derivations, transformations etc in the SLT System.

Suppose the client has a requirement to view Purchase Requisitions data in near realtime in the BW Reports. Here they want to see the Amount fields in the local currency but the EBAN Table has the Amounts in the Document currency. Therefore, you will need to convert the source currency to the target currency but you do not want to enhance the standard table due to some constraints. Also, we cannot use the ODP datasource as a source for SLT.

In such scenarios, you can take advantage of the Advanced Replication Settings in SLT.

Step 1:

Add the required field exchange rate in the target table in LTRS.

You can do this by going to Table Settings->Table Structure->Add Field

Once you have added the required fields, save the table structure.

 

Step 2:

Begin by creating an Include Program in the SLT system:

In this Include program, you need to call a Remote Enabled Function Module from the source system. Here we are assuming the FM that you want to call is already Remote Enabled.

Next,in order to call the Remote Enabled FM, you need to know the technical name of the RFC destination.

You may hardcode this based on the environment of your SLT system.

Now we call the Remote Enabled FM by passing the FM name followed by the RFC destination.

Below is the sample code of the Include Program to derive the exchange rate:

Here 'BAPI_EXCHANGERATE_GETDETAIL'  is a Remote Enabled Function Module

*******************************************************************************************

DATA: ls_exchrate TYPE bapi1093_0,
             lv_rfcdest   type sysid.

lv_rfcdest = sy-sysid.

CASE lv_rfcdest.
  WHEN 'SLTD'.
    lv_rfcdest = 'ECCD'.
  WHEN 'SLTQ'.
    lv_rfcdest = 'ECCQ’.
  WHEN 'SLTP'.
    lv_rfcdest = 'ECCP’
ENDCASE.

*Remote call from SLT to ECC getting exchange rate

*The field symbols follow a generic naming convention <wa_s_tablename> for source and <wa_r_tablename> for target work areas, where tablename denotes the source table name. For instance, the work areas for the table SPFLI would be <WA_S_SPFLI> and <WA_R_SPFLI>.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_EXCHANGERATE_GETDETAIL' DESTINATION lv_rfcdest
  EXPORTING
    rate_type  = 'M'
    from_curr  = <wa_s_eban>-waers        
    to_currncy = 'USD'
    date       = <wa_s_eban>-badat
  IMPORTING
    exch_rate  = ls_exchrate.
*   RETURN           =

*assigned to EBAN LT structure
<wa_r_eban>-zzexch_rate = ls_exchrate-exch_rate.

 

Now that you have written the Include Program, you need to call it.

Step 3: Open LTRS t-code in the SLT system and navigate to Rule Assignment under Advanced Replication Settings.

Right click on the Rule Assignment and enter your table name.

Step 4: Click on the Create Icon and select Event Based rule since we want to execute the custom include program that we have written.

We will select the Begin of Record (BOR) event since we want to access the data of the source and target work areas.

Double click on the event and insert the Include Name in the Include Name field.

Step 5: Now change your event status from New to Released and save it.

Step 6: In your target system table e.g. in the BW system add the field for Exchange Rate in your staging adso and replicate your datasource so that it reflects the newly added exchange rate field that we added in the target table structure in SLT. Map the datasource field with the staging adso field and start the dataload.

 

You can see that the Exchange Rate column is populated. You can validate the values by running the FM in the ECC system for few records.

 

Now you may ask, what if my standard Function Module is not Remote Enabled in the source system.

E.g. you want to convert the WBS Element number from Internal to External Format and the FM

CONVERSION_EXIT_ABPSP_OUTPUT is not Remote Enabled.

In this case, you can proceed with creating a copy of the normal function module and make the copy as Remote Enabled.

If the Type of the Import and Export Parameters is not specified in the Standard Function Module, specify it in the copy Function Module else you might run into a syntax error.

Once you are done creating your Remote Enabled Function Module in the source system, proceed with creating the Include Program by following the steps mentioned earlier and you are good to go.

Feel free to reach out to me for any clarifications and I would be happy to help.

Happy learning!

entry.title: Re: Develop ALV report based on user input
entry.content: Dear Mahesh

entry.title: Re: Trigger an outbound Idoc when a PR is created.
entry.content: Hi Raymond, everythings done, Now, the last thing that is remaining is to attach this Idoc to the PR in 'Relationships' of Idoc, any Idea or suggested content ?
